meta.title: inflation.mk — Your personal inflation
meta.description: Build your own basket and see your personal inflation rate for North Macedonia, using official data from the State Statistical Office.

hero.subtitle: Most people don't spend like the national average — where food alone is 40% of the basket. Set how you actually spend and see your own inflation rate.
hero.vsEqual: in line with the official rate

chart.title: How your inflation tracked the official rate
chart.subtitle: Your current basket, applied to the last 10 years of official prices, next to the official rate.

breakdown.title: Inflation by category
breakdown.intro: Official price and pay trends in North Macedonia over the last 5 years.
breakdown.subtitle: How prices in each part of the official basket have moved over the last 5 years, next to the headline rate. Sorted by the latest year-on-year change.

wages.title: Wages vs inflation
wages.subtitle: Year-over-year growth of the average net wage, next to the official inflation rate. When the solid line is above the dashed one, pay outpaced prices.

basket.title: Your basket
basket.hint: Each slider starts at the average household — drag left or right to match how you spend, then set your monthly budget to see each share in real money. Shares are relative and always add up to 100%.

methodology.title: How it works
methodology.body1: Official inflation uses a single basket for the whole country, in which food alone is about 40%. Your spending almost certainly looks different.
methodology.body2: Set how much of your spending goes to each category. We take each category's official year-over-year price change and weight it by your share to get your personal rate.
methodology.body3: By default, essentials — food, housing, transport, health and so on — make up about 75% of the basket, and quality-of-life spending — eating out, recreation, holidays — about 25%. Adjust freely, or load the official national weights to compare.

footer.attribution: Source: <link>State Statistical Office of the Republic of North Macedonia</link>.
footer.sourceName: State Statistical Office of the Republic of North Macedonia
footer.note: An independent project. Not affiliated with the State Statistical Office.
